title: The Insidious Cost of 'AI Brain Fry'
description: A Harvard Business Review study uncovers the stressful downside of artificial-intelligence tools at work.
content:
“I end each day exhausted,” engineer Francesco Bonacci of Cua AI admitted in a recent X post, “not from the work itself, but from the managing of the work.”
A new study in the Harvard Business Review confirmed that “cognitive exhaustion” from rigorou... [1027 chars]

title: IUST faculty bags major research grants from JKST&IC
description: Excelsior Correspondent AWANTIPORA, Mar 8: Faculty at the Islamic University of Science and Technology (IUST), Awantipora, has secured significant research funding under the Sponsored Research & Extension Programme (SREP 2025-26) of the Jammu and Kashmir Science, Technology and Innovation Council (JKST&IC), Department of Science & Technology, Government of Jammu & Kashmir. The approved projects span a wide spectrum of emerging and critical research areas including artificial intelligence, healthcare technology, food safety, environmental monitoring and sustainable livelihoods. The funded studies include […]

title: Pakistan’s equipment, information, AI is from a different country: Army Chief
description: ‘Army contributing significantly to Ladakh’s economy’ Sanjeev Pargal JAMMU, Mar 8: Army chief Gen Upendra Dwivedi has said when a Pakistan soldier presses a button , the equipment, the information and the Artificial Intelligence (AI) is from a different country which means the interoperability is a cause of concern. He said the Indian Army has contributed significantly to economy of the Union Territory of Ladakh, much more than the tourists. Speaking at the podcast, the Army Chief quoted Russia-Ukraine example […]

title: Journalist, AI insider Karen Hao at Idea Exchange: ‘The guardrails will come from the people, they are finding a reason to push back against the AI empire’
description: Journalist and AI insider Karen Hao on what is most troubling about Artificial General Intelligence, why data centres are unsustainable, how safety and accountability mean different things in the AI world and why her book, Empire of AI, is not kind to OpenAI CEO Sam Altman. The session was moderated by Amitabh Sinha, Deputy Editor, The Indian Express
content:
Amitabh Sinha: What are the alternative pathways that this technology could have taken?

title: Revealed: How wealthy Brits are using social media for massive investment decisions
description: New research which surveyed Britons earning £100,000 or more about their investment choices suggests many high earners rely on social media and Artificial Intelligence for advice.
content:
Nearly one third of high-earning Brits admit they use social media to make major investment decisions, with video-sharing app TikTok emerging as the most popular platform for financial guidance.

title: We must stop AI reversing progress women have made in workplace
description: Few chief executives are treating inclusion as part of their strategic response to the rise of artificial intelligence, writes the new women in finance champion
content:
Britain can congratulate itself on progress for women in business. Women now hold more than 42 per cent of board seats across FTSE 350 companies — a figure that would have seemed implausible when I began my career in banking.
